rule: connector between rows = parent-child. Parent slot (top3) gets LAST indicator. Children fill expansion.
meta: dp spotted the connector as structural cue

rule: multiple expansion groups. Each parent gets last indicator of its child group.
meta: two sub-groups with different border colors

rule: parent slot gets the indicator that MATCHES THE BORDER COLOR of its expansion box
meta: requires correct color perception. Andy's color fix was essential.

rule: border color can occupy MULTIPLE parent slots. Count of border-color palette items = parent slot count.
meta: two blues in palette = two blue parent slots

rule: SWAP mechanic (not pick-place). Container box gets child identities in INDICATOR READING ORDER. Each child box gets its content pair.
meta: paradigm shift: pick-place → swap. dp: indicator order determines container contents.

rule: each box = [own_color, CHILD_BOX_COLOR, own_color]. Palindrome indicators encode nesting depth.
meta: russian nesting dolls. center of palindrome = innermost leaf.

rule: each box gets its color group + OTHER box's identity in last position. Mutual acknowledgment.

description: L1-5 use pick-and-place. L6+ uses swap. Structural rules (hierarchy, border=identity, indicator order) transfer across paradigms. Only the interaction mechanic changes.
8 levels teach: flat→hierarchy→multi-group→border-identity→multi-parent→swap-grid→palindrome-nesting→cross-reference. Each level builds capacity required by the next.
The indicator bar is ALWAYS the source of truth. Spatial arrangement of boxes is presentation; indicator left-to-right sequence is semantics. When in doubt, follow indicator order.
Border color on a box = that box's identity. The indicator matching the border color goes in the parent/identity slot of that box.
